summary: Merged a broad ground/stage decomp pass over remaining gr units, especially grpushon, grpura, and grgreens. The PR added matched or closer-matching C for previously stubbed/partial routines, corrected stage-specific Ground union types and headers, and split a grgreens data symbol in symbols.txt. The decomp.dev bot reported +1960 matched code bytes, +32 matched data bytes, 8 new matches, and 17 unmatched-item improvements, with two small grgreens regressions.
what_changed: Implemented and/or improved multiple Pokemon/Pura stage functions in src/melee/gr/grpura.c, including grPura_80211D00, grPura_802120E0, grPura_8021231C, grPura_80212CD4, grPura_80212EF4, grPura_80212FC0, grPura_80213030, and grPura_80213250.

Added typed Pura data in grpura.h by replacing extern u16 grPu_803E6C0C[266] with GrPuVtxMapEntry grPu_803E6C0C[42], allowing grPura_80212FC0 and grPura_80213030 to use named x00/x02/x04/x08 fields instead of raw pointer arithmetic and M2C_FIELD.

Updated src/melee/gr/types.h for Pura ground variables: xC4 became s16, xC8 in grPura_GroundVars became s16 instead of HSD_JObj*, and a new grPura_GroundVars3 stores HSD_JObj* xC4[25] plus CmSubject* x128[25].

Implemented grPushOn_80218888 and fn_802192A4 in src/melee/gr/grpushon.c. The new PushOn logic computes fighter-relative light distances, sorts light indices, hides extra light objects, positions point/spot lights, blends colors, and checks collision/region bounds for fn_802192A4.
Changed grpushon.h prototype for fn_802192A4 from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to s32 fn_802192A4(void*, HSD_GObj*, s32*).

Improved grgreens matching by adding static s16 grGr_803E787C[30], splitting the previous grGr_803E787C data symbol into grGr_803E787C size 0x3C and grGr_803E78B8 size 0x88 in config/GALE01/symbols.txt.

Adjusted grgreens block indexing through getBlock using a 2D array cast, swapped i/j use in grGreens_802159B8, rewrote grGreens_80215D54 around getBlock swaps, changed grGreens_80214FA8 to use grGr_803E787C for mpJointSetCb2, and reordered row weighting in grGreens_802166C4.
Rewrote grGreens_80214B58 from a static indices array loop to explicit BOMB calls backed by a static inline BOMB helper, preserving the bomb-index order while improving match quality.
Included additional dependencies needed by the new code, including baselib/debug.h, cm/types.h, lb/lbvector.h, and math_ppc.h.
smart_moves: Converting grPu_803E6C0C from a raw u16 array to GrPuVtxMapEntry made the stride and embedded HSD_JObj* explicit, reducing unsafe M2C_FIELD usage while improving nearby matches.

Adding grPura_GroundVars3 to the Ground union captured the real per-stage state shape for camera subjects and jobj arrays, which made grPura_80212CD4 and grPura_80212EF4 expressible as normal indexed loops.
The grgreens getBlock helper uses a typed 2D array cast over gp->gv.greens.x8_blocks, a compact way to express row/column indexing without leaving magic byte offsets in gameplay logic.
The PR used exact constants where matching required them, such as 3.4028235e38f instead of F32_MAX and inline 0.8f/3600.0f camera/background interpolation constants.
The explicit BOMB helper plus unrolled call sequence in grGreens_80214B58 preserved compiler ordering better than the cleaner static-index-array loop.
The noinline wrapper chain grPura_80211E08_noinline and grPura_80211E08_noinline2 appears to be a deliberate call-shape tactic for grPura_802125F0 matching.
Replacing ad hoc __assert calls with HSD_ASSERT line-number macros in grpura.c aligned with project debugging conventions while preserving expected line metadata.
decomp_lessons: When an apparent flat array is really a table with pointer fields, introduce a small typed struct before trying to match users; it can eliminate M2C_FIELD casts and reveal loop bounds.
Stage Ground union variants may need multiple per-object interpretations for the same stage family; Pura needed separate pura, pura2, and pura3 layouts.

For grgreens-style grid data, source-level row/column order matters. The PR changed several calls from getBlock(gp, i, j) to getBlock(gp, j, i), suggesting earlier decomp used the wrong logical orientation in at least one routine.
Do not assume symbolic convenience constants match codegen. Literal max-float values and literal camera constants may be required for exact output.
Readable table loops can regress matching compared with explicit repeated calls; unrolling a small fixed sequence is sometimes the right decomp tactic.

Data symbol boundaries in symbols.txt may need to be revisited after arrays are identified. Splitting a 0xC4 object into a 0x3C array plus a following 0x88 object enabled better grgreens data accounting.
Header prototypes should be tightened as soon as signatures are known; this PR replaced several UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS declarations with void or concrete s32 signatures.
naming_conventions: Preserved stage-prefix naming for known-address functions: grPura_802xxxxx, grPushOn_802xxxxx, grGreens_802xxxxx, and local fn_802xxxxx where no semantic name is known.
Used data-symbol prefixes matching the stage unit, such as grPu_803E6C0C, grPu_803E7620, grGr_803E787C, and grPushOn_803B844C.
New struct name GrPuVtxMapEntry follows the stage abbreviation plus purpose pattern, while unknown fields remain offset-style x00, x02, x04, x08.
Ground variable structs retain stage-specific names such as grPura_GroundVars, grPura_GroundVars2, grPura_GroundVars3, and grGreens_BlockVars.
Temporary noinline helpers were named by appending _noinline and _noinline2 to the wrapped function name, making the matching tactic visible rather than giving it false semantics.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK and UNUSED locals in grpura.c and grpushon.c to shape stack frames and register allocation.
Used noinline wrapper functions around grPura_80211E08 to alter call generation in grPura_802125F0.
Used exact floating-point literals such as 3.4028235e38f, 0.8f, 3600.0f, 30.0f, -30.0f, and threshold values like 0.01f/10.0f/50.0f/100.0f.
Replaced a compact static index table in grGreens_80214B58 with a small inline BOMB helper and explicit calls to match instruction/order behavior.
Used a typed 2D array cast in getBlock: ((struct grGreens_BlockVars(*)[6]) gp->gv.greens.x8_blocks)[i][j].
Used selection-sort style loops in grPushOn_80218888 to order light indices by computed distance, matching the observed fixed-size behavior.
Used union_type_dobj checks when recursively walking HSD_JObj trees in grPura_80213250 before accessing jobj->u.dobj.
Adjusted symbols.txt data boundaries for grGr_803E787C and grGr_803E78B8 to reflect newly identified arrays.
review_feedback: No human review comments or PR body were present in the slice, so reviewer intent is not available.
The decomp.dev bot reported matched code increased to 69.18% with +1960 bytes and matched data increased to 41.19% with +32 bytes.

The bot listed 8 new matches: grpushon fn_802192A4, grpura grPura_80212EF4, grpura grPura_8021231C, grgreens fn_80216DE4, grpura .sdata, grpura grPura_80212FC0, grpura grPura_80213030, and grpura grPura_80211D00.
The bot listed major unmatched improvements including grPushOn_80218888 to 92.87%, grGreens_80214B58 to 99.26%, grPura_80212CD4 to 69.10%, and grPura_802120E0 to 56.17%.
The bot also reported two small grgreens regressions: grGreens_80215ED8 decreased by 12 bytes and fn_80215B84 decreased by 6 bytes.
follow_up_queries: Check whether the two bot-reported grgreens regressions in grGreens_80215ED8 and fn_80215B84 were resolved by later PRs.
Look for later semantic renames of GrPuVtxMapEntry fields x00/x02/x04/x06/x08 and Pura ground-var fields xC4/xC6/xC8.
Search for other grgreens block-grid routines that should use the same typed 2D getBlock row/column convention.
Compare later grPushOn_80218888 work for possible semantic names around light sorting, point_light, spot_light, and grPushOn_803B844C.
Verify whether grPura_80211E08_noinline/noinline2 remained as permanent matching scaffolding or was removed after adjacent functions matched.
